agent1 (Round 1 summarized): The suitable diagnostic method is a postero-buccolingual oriented periapical film using the bisecting-angle technique. This provides clear images necessary for identifying small lesions between teeth in pediatric patients effectively.
The appropriate imaging modality involves utilizing high-resolution digital lateral cephalograms that offer comprehensive views including both maxillary molars' mesial surfaces while minimizing radiation exposure through optimized positioning techniques tailored towards younger subjects. To summarize:

Posterior interproximal caries are optimally detected by bite-wing radiography, especially when applying the bisecting angle technique for enhanced clarity. High-resolution digital lateral cephalograms also provide valuable information but may not surpass the precision offered by targeted bite-wings under specific conditions. Sentence count: 3 \boxed{-}

To refine further within two-sentence constraints focusing strictly on critical aspects related directly back to diagnosing posterior interproximal caries efficiently,

Optimized use of bite-wing radiographs employs the bisecting angle technique crucially aiding detection; alternatively, high-quality digital lateral cephalograms complementarily contribute essential anatomical details without exceeding necessity or overexposure risks typically associated broader scans like full-mouth series preferred more generally yet less precise here. 

Sentence Count: 2 \boxed{\text{}}
Certainly! Here’s a summary adhering to your instructions:

Bite-wing radiographs, preferably taken with the bisecting angle technique, are ideal for diagnosing posterior interproximal caries due to their superior ability to visualize these areas clearly. Digital lateral cephalograms can supplement this but are not as specialized for pinpointing such subtle lesions compared to focused bite wings.
